iterate_target: Leaderboard walk-forward champion on ETH/1h: kama_stoch_pullback_v1_8to1 (QScore 4.27, PF 1.857, DD 10.1, 42 trades)
thesis_hint: Iterate on a passing strategy this cycle. Recent history is still explore-heavy enough to preserve the soft 70/30 doctrine, and the most overused recent lane is 4h exploration plus ETH channel discussion. Rotate away from both by exploiting a different supported passing family: ETH 1h kama_stoch_pullback, not ETH channel shortbias and not ETH 4h tail harvester. Demand 2-3 focused variants only: one should reduce drawdown from the 10.1% baseline without collapsing the 42-trade density, one should improve pullback quality by tightening trend-state context before entry, and an optional third may improve exit efficiency after weak bounce follow-through if it preserves the family's trade count. Do not drift into channel-failure shorts, do not broaden to BTC transfer tests, and do not spray tiny threshold edits around one trigger.
stop_condition: Stop the batch when either (A) one variant improves the current pass with walk-forward QScore >= 4.40, profit_factor >= 1.90, total_trades >= 38, and max_drawdown_pct <= 9.0, or (B) 3 focused variants are exhausted without beating the current ETH/1h kama_stoch_pullback baseline. Abort any variant with total_trades < 30, profit_factor < 1.30, or max_drawdown_pct > 12.0.
rotation_reason: Allow exploitation this cycle: recent history remains explore-heavy, no last-3 or last-5 concentration rule forces another asset rotation, and ETH/1h kama_stoch_pullback is a supported walk-forward pass that rotates away from the more overused 4h lane and ETH channel family.
